:::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} ## Prison Population
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Prison Population Trends

This section provides a comprehensive look at the dynamics of Nevada's prison admissions, releases, and population, highlighting key aspects such as admission types, demographic breakdowns, criminal offenses, and sentence lengths.


``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset} ### Number of Admissions ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Sentence about change in admissions from 2010 to 2020.


``` ::: ::: ### Number of Releases ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Sentence about change in releases from 2010 to 2020.


``` ::: ::: ### Year-End Population ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Sentence about change in year-end population from 2010 to 2020.


``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Admission Types

Prison populations are shaped by two primary groups: new court commitments, or individuals newly incarcerated for a criminal offense, and parole returns and revocations, those who return to prison for failing to meet the conditions of their parole.

In Nevada in 2020, people in prison for new court commitments accounted for 92% of the prison population, while parole returns and revocations accounted for 8%.


``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Demographics

``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset} ### Race and Ethinicity ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, White, non-Hispanic individuals made up the largest portion of people in prison for both new court commitments and parole returns and revocations.

``` ::: ::: ### Gender ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, male individuals made up the largest portion of people in prison for both new court commitments and parole returns and revocations.

``` ::: ::: ### Age ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, individuals aged 25-34 years made up the largest portion of people in prison for both new court commitments and parole returns and revocations.

``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Sentence Length

Sentence about most common sentence length.



``` ::: :::

## Parole Eligibility
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

How is Parole Eligibility Determined?

Only felons are eligible for parole in Nevada because they are the only offenders sent to prison. A person sentenced for a crime committed on or after July 1, 1995 may be paroled when they have served the minimum term. Each felony sentence has a minimum and maximum term unless a definite term is required by statute. If an individual is sentenced to consecutive terms of imprisonment, the court must aggregate the minimum terms of imprisonment to determine release eligibility. If an individual is sentenced to concurrent terms of imprisonment, eligibility for parole occurs after the longest of the minimum terms has been served. In some cases, good time may be deducted from the minimum term and hasten parole eligibility.


``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Prison Population Currently Eligible for Parole

Show breakdown of types:
1) New court commitments/1-24.99 years
2) New court commitments/<1 & >= 25 years
3) Parole returns/1-24.99 years
4) Parole returns/<1 & >= 25 years


``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Focusing on New Court Commitments with
Sentences of 1 to 24.99 Years

For a nuanced analysis, this section narrows the focus to individuals serving sentences of 1 to 24.99 years for new court commitments.

In 2020, there were 1,766 people who were in prison and currently eligible for parole. This group made up 27% of the prison population in prison for new court commitments with sentences between 1 to 24.99 years.



``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Demographics

``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, among the prison population currently eligible for parole, Black, non-Hispanic individuals constituted the majority, representing 36 percent.


``` ::: ::: ### Gender ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, among the prison population currently eligible for parole, males constituted the majority, representing 91 percent.


``` ::: ::: ### Age ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

In 2020, among the prison population currently eligible for parole, people between the ages of 25-34 years constituted the majority, representing 39 percent.


``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Sentence Lengths

In 2020, among the prison population currently eligible for parole, people with sentences between 2 to 4.9 years years constituted the majority, representing 35 percent.



``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Criminal Offenses

In 2020, among the prison population currently eligible for parole, people with property offenses constituted the majority, representing 29 percent.


``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Missing Data

This section will have information about missing parole eligibility data for the state once new NCRP data is released.
``` ::: :::
